overview: The Wausau School Board held its regular June 2026 meeting, approving seven motions covering the preliminary 2026-27 budget, employee handbook updates, meal price changes, athletics/activities code revisions, policy updates, an HVAC/roofing contractor selection, and a debt defeasance strategy that will bring the district's total long-term interest savings to just over $29 million. The meeting also featured presentations on the art department's three-year goals and the district's summer learning programs.
presiding: Lance Trupop (implied board chair, based on leading meeting and calling motions)

agenda.item: Roll Call and Pledge of Allegiance
Art Department Presentation - Three-Year Goals and Curriculum Alignment
Consent Agenda - Personnel, Minutes, Bills, and Donations
Motion: Paid Meal Price Update for 2026-27
Motion: Athletics and Activities Code Revisions
Motion: Policy 9270 - Home-Based Private or Tribal Schooling
Motion: Policy 2431.01 - High School Activities Code
Motion: Rearview HVAC and Roofing Project - Myron Construction
2026-27 Debt Defeasance Strategy Presentation and Adoption

discussions.item: Art Department Presentation - Three-Year Goals and Curriculum Alignment
discussions.body: The district's art coordinator, in her second year, presented the art department's three-year goals focused on amplifying arts reach, creating shared K-12 skill experiences, and meaningful vertical alignment between grade levels. She described the creation of an art continuum task force involving elementary, middle, and high school educators who identified priority standards, and a department-wide book study on teaching meaning in art making. Board member Lance Trupop expressed support, noting the value of consistent experiences as students move into middle school. The coordinator also announced an upcoming partnership with the Leigh Yawkey Woodson Art Museum to give art educators opportunities to make art themselves.

discussions.item: Consent Agenda - Personnel, Minutes, Bills, and Donations
discussions.body: The consent agenda included personnel actions, minutes from the May 11 and May 18, 2026 sessions, payment of bills, budget status, investment report, and donations from multiple community organizations including CW Fly Bags, Hub Central Collision, King Buffet of Wausau, Lee Insurance, Peace United Church of Christ, Safe Kids of Marathon County Coalition, Wausau Breakfast Optimist Club, ENT and Allergy Associates, and Cloverbeltt Credit Union. A motion was made, seconded, and approved unanimously with no discussion.

Elizabeth Channel, Assistant Superintendent of Operations, had previously presented the preliminary budget at the May committee meeting. The board was asked to approve the initial budget to allow 2026-27 expenditures to proceed before final budgets are set. The motion was made, seconded, and passed unanimously with no board discussion at this meeting.
Tabitha Gundrum had presented employee handbook updates at the May committee meeting. The board approved the recommended changes unanimously with no additional discussion at this meeting.
Karen Fox had previously presented proposed meal price changes at the May committee meeting. The board approved the proposed changes unanimously with no discussion.

Dr. John Tomsky and BJ Brandt had presented suggested changes to the athletic and activities code at the May committee meeting. The board approved the revised code unanimously with no additional discussion.

discussions.item: Policy 9270 - Home-Based Private or Tribal Schooling and Policy 2431.01 - High School Activities Code
discussions.body: Dr. John Tomsky had presented proposed changes to both policies at the May committee meeting. Both policies were moved, seconded, and approved unanimously in separate votes with no board discussion.

discussions.item: Rearview HVAC and Roofing Project - Myron Construction Corporation
discussions.body: Ryan Armansky, director of buildings and grounds, had presented this project at the May committee meeting. The board approved moving forward with Myron Construction Corporation as the general contractor unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: 2026-27 Debt Defeasance Strategy Presentation and Adoption
discussions.body: Eric Cass from PMA Financial presented the annual debt defeasance strategy, explaining that the district would place excess levy funds into an irrevocable escrow trust targeting the longest-maturity, highest-coupon debt from a 2022 issuance, eliminating nearly three years of debt service through 2039-2040 and generating approximately $9.6 million in savings. Combined with prior years' defeasances dating back to 2017-18, total long-term interest savings would reach just over $29 million. Board member Nick Casher asked whether repeatedly prepaying debt affects the district's standing with bond investors; Cass explained that the callable feature is disclosed upfront and investors price in the possibility of early redemption. Another board member noted the strategy also helps smooth mill rate fluctuations and generates additional state equalization aid, projected at $5.7 million, though Cass cautioned that figure carries volatility. The motion was approved unanimously.

Rob Phelps, principal at Horace Mann and summer learning coordinator, and Vicki Christensen, out-of-school learning coordinator, began presenting the district's summer learning programs. Phelps described the morning elementary program, structured as 'transition to' grade-level programs focusing on math, ELA, and encore rotations, and a middle school transition program combining school forest visits with academic intervention. The transcript ends mid-presentation before the full update was completed.

publicComment: No public comment was offered. The board noted that a written comment submission option exists on the district website but encouraged residents to email the board directly instead.
actionItems: Approved preliminary 2026-27 budget to allow expenditures to proceed before final budget adoption
Approved 2026-27 paid meal price changes as presented by Karen Fox
Approved revised athletics and activities code as presented by Dr. Tomsky and BJ Brandt
Approved proposed changes to Policy 9270 (home-based private or tribal schooling)
Approved proposed changes to Policy 2431.01 (High School Activities Code)
Approved Myron Construction Corporation as general contractor for the Rearview HVAC and roofing project
Adopted 2026-27 debt defeasance strategy targeting long-maturity 2022 bond issuance, projected to save approximately $9.6 million and bring cumulative district savings to over $29 million
Approved consent agenda including personnel actions, May 11 and May 18 meeting minutes, bills, and donations from multiple community organizations
Art department to proceed with partnership with Leigh Yawkey Woodson Art Museum for educator professional development in art making
title: Regular Meeting - 2026-06-08

Proposed changes to Policy 2431.01 (High School Activities Code)
